topic: EXP-271 Falsifiability module remediation — integration drift (patches present in FUSE workspace, ABSENT from git canonical)
trigger_note: Decider report 2026-04-28T01:28 declared EXP-271 INTEGRATED with 4 patches applied and 3501 tests passing, then pushed queue_id 280 for adversarial review. On reading the curmudgeon's fresh git clone of main, NONE of the four patches are visible: build-scripts/generate-html.js still has class="falsifiability-module" (count: 1) and zero occurrences of ds-falsifiability-module; test.js has zero EXP-271 assertions; docs/index.html still serves the unprefixed legacy module. The same file paths in the FUSE workspace mount DO carry the EXP-271 patches (ds-falsifiability-module count 2 in generate-html.js, EXP-271 assertion count 22 in test.js). The integration is stranded in FUSE.
current_verdict_holds_explanation: The decider's INTEGRATED verdict does not hold against git's canonical state. The published site (GitHub Pages) is built from git, so the live readers see the OLD unprefixed module with the OLD asymmetric framing and the OLD 'sub-meter precision' factual error. ISS-1631..ISS-1638 were closed prematurely.
recommendation_summary: Re-run the EXP-271 integration end-to-end in a clean clone and push (operator rescue if decider push keeps failing). After the rescue lands, the curmudgeon should re-review the integrated content under a new queue_id; this review covers the integration-drift finding only. The patches as visible in FUSE are correctly constructed and should land cleanly when the push succeeds — see fuse_side_patch_review below for the substantive assessment.

fuse_workspace_state.docs/index.html (workspace): NOTE: Even FUSE has stale docs/index.html — the source files were patched but build.js never regenerated the artifact. So if the rescue lifts FUSE source files into git verbatim, docs/index.html will still need a rebuild + commit.

drift_detection.decider_daily_report_claim: monitor/decisions/daily-report-2026-04-28T01:28.json reports exp_271_integration with 4 patches applied, 3501 tests passing, 8 ISS-163x issues closed, and queue push of overview-falsification-EXP-271 (qid 280). The test count + 'INTEGRATED' verdict match what would have been seen inside the decider's clone, but the commit never reached origin/main.
drift_detection.workspace_sync_constraint: build-scripts/generate-html.js and test.js are listed in the workspace-sync NEVER_PUSH set (per CLAUDE.md and the workspace-sync prompt). Universal-pusher mode rescues runtime data files but explicitly does NOT round-trip source code from FUSE. So the EXP-271 source patches will NOT auto-recover; an operator rescue or a fresh decider run is required.
drift_detection.related_pattern: Commit 5a29442 (2026-04-27 19:48) 'Operator rescue: ship generate-html.js drift (EXP-255/256/257/261/264)' shows the exact same drift pattern was rescued less than 6 hours before EXP-271 was integrated. EXP-271 is the next manifestation of the same class of failure.

holes_found.description: EXP-271 integration is not in git canonical. The decider's daily-report-2026-04-28T01:28.json declared INTEGRATED and pushed queue_id 280 for review, but build-scripts/generate-html.js, test.js, and docs/index.html in origin/main still reflect the pre-EXP-271 state. The patches exist only in the FUSE workspace mount, where workspace-sync is forbidden from pushing them (NEVER_PUSH list). All eight ISS-163x issues that were closed against this integration (ISS-1631..ISS-1638) are effectively still live on the published site: WCAG contrast not bumped, framing still asymmetric, classes still unprefixed, denominator caption not added, 17 new tests not running, eg-falsifiability still says 'sub-meter precision' (the 600× factually-incorrect wording), 'pending' status still asymmetric, visual order still has falsifiability module ahead of bar chart.
holes_found.recommendation: Operator rescue: from a clean clone, copy FUSE's build-scripts/generate-html.js and test.js verbatim, run `node build.js` to regenerate docs/index.html with the patched module, run `node test.js` to confirm the 3501-test count restored, commit + push. After the rescue, re-open ISS-1631..ISS-1638 momentarily (or confirm closure-in-name-only) and have the decider re-close them with the rescue commit SHA on closure_evidence. Add a follow-up issue: the decider's push-failure block should either (a) hard-fail the integration claim if the push exits non-zero, or (b) emit a high-priority signal in monitor/decisions/attention-inbox.json so the next decider run notices the drift. The 6-hour-prior 5a29442 rescue suggests this drift class is recurring and deserves a structural mitigation rather than serial rescues.

holes_found.description: Even within FUSE, docs/index.html has not been rebuilt to reflect the source patches. grep -c 'ds-falsifiability-module' on the FUSE docs/index.html returns 0, and 'class="falsifiability-module"' (legacy) returns 1. So the FUSE artifact is internally inconsistent — source has been edited but the build artifact lags. Any operator rescue that just `cp`s FUSE → git for source files will leave docs/index.html with the OLD module rendered against the NEW test assertions, and `node test.js` will fail loudly.
holes_found.recommendation: The rescue must include a `node build.js` step to regenerate docs/index.html in the clean clone after lifting the FUSE source files. Verify the same 3501 passing test count the decider reported. Do not skip the rebuild step.

holes_found.description: Decider's 2026-04-28T01:28 daily report contains exp_271_integration.tests = '3501 passed, 0 failed'. But the FUSE-side test.js adds 17 new EXP-271 assertions beyond the prior baseline. Tinker / integrity have observed 3480 / 3476 pre-EXP-271 elsewhere, suggesting the decider's 3501 count is plausible (3480 + 17 ≈ 3497, close to 3501 with some other concurrent additions). This is consistent — only flagging because the decider's daily report is now the only audit trail of a run that didn't actually persist, and the 3501 number cannot be reproduced from origin/main.
holes_found.recommendation: After rescue, log the rebuilt test count to a new daily report and reference the rescue commit SHA. The decider should also adopt a post-push verification step ('git log -1 origin/main' compared to local HEAD before declaring INTEGRATED).

fuse_side_patch_review.note: Substantive adversarial assessment of the EXP-271 patches as visible in FUSE workspace, on the assumption that the integration drift will be resolved. This is the review the curmudgeon would deliver if the patches were in git.

observations: Visual order thesis-hero → bar chart → ds-falsifiability-module is correct per ISS-1638 and matches EXP-256 proposal intent.
Class renaming to ds-falsifiability-module / ds-fm-* is consistent with the codebase's ds- prefix convention (ds-tab-bar, ds-scorecard, ds-verdict-bars, ds-thesis-hero, ds-evaluate-preface).

${counts.total} resolves to 69 (not 71), because counts.total = baseWins.length where baseWins = wins.filter(w=>/^\d{3}$/.test(w.id)) — the 3-digit-only filter excludes the 057b/058b sub-IDs. So the patched caption 'Four conditions assessed against the dome's ${counts.total} claimed wins' renders as 'the dome's 69 claimed wins'. ISS-1634 IS properly mitigated; the audit_findings claim is accurate.
Aria-labels carry counts.total too, so screen-reader users hear '0 of 69 claims met' which matches the visible '0 / 69 met'. Consistent.

Sub-status disambiguators '2 attempted · 0 succeed' (Cond 1), 'imports globe radius' (Cond 3), 'OPEN-001 · dome-acknowledged' (Cond 4) preserve ISS-1637's distinction between 'didn't attempt' and 'attempted-and-failed' without sacrificing the visual symmetry of the headline column.
p1_hero_block.kernel_of_truth_for_dome_defender: A defender could argue that condition 4 should remain visually distinct from conditions 1 and 3 because the dome itself acknowledges OPEN-001 as work-in-progress and the asymmetric pending grey was the more honest treatment. The EXP's response is: empirically '0 / 69 claims meet the bar today' is true regardless of acknowledgement status, and the OPEN-001 sub-status preserves the open-acknowledgement nuance. The trade — visual consistency vs. pending-grey concession — is defensible and the sub-status preserves the lost information.

observations: Bumping ds-fm-status-num to 1.2rem (≈19.2px bold) crosses the WCAG AA large-text threshold (14pt bold = 18.67px), allowing the 3:1 contrast ratio (rather than 4.5:1) to apply for --refuted-solid (#d46a6a) and --ink-3 (#8a8680) over --card-bg (#222240) in dark mode. The audit_findings.wcag_calculation_detail's ratios (~4.43:1 for refuted, ~4.23:1 for ink-3) are consistent with sRGB luminance arithmetic; both clear 3:1 large-text but neither cleared 4.5:1 small-text — the bump is the cleanest mitigation.
Switching ds-fm-status-sub from --ink-3 to --ink-2 (#b8b5ad ~7.65:1) clears AA at the 0.72rem small-text threshold even before the 1.2rem bump. Defensible.

Mobile breakpoint reduces status-num to 1.1rem (~17.6px) — close to the 14pt-bold large-text threshold (18.67px). 1.1rem bold is borderline; recommend running a screenshot pass on the integrated build to verify the mobile bold weight reads as ≥14pt, but not blocking — the rescue test harness should catch this if it does fail.

Site-wide --refuted-solid and --ink-3 dark-mode contrast remains a known issue per the audit; this EXP correctly scopes the fix to the module rather than touching design tokens. Future cleanup EXP is appropriate.

p3_factual_fix.arithmetic_audit: Claim: 'WIN-057's best result (6.2% RMSE) is 600× worse than standard geodesy's sub-0.01% precision over similar distances'. Math check: 6.2% on a 10,000 km route ≈ 620 km error. 0.01% on the same route ≈ 1 km error. 620 / 1 = 620× ≈ 600×. The 600× ratio is geodesy precision near 1 km (sub-0.01%), NOT 1 m (sub-meter, which would be 620,000×). The original 'sub-meter precision' wording was off by ~1000× and the new wording matches the data/wins.json WIN-057 finding line which already says '(sub-0.01%)'. Internally consistent.

p4_test_block.assertions_review: 17 new assertions cover: presence of ds-falsifiability-module class and CSS rule, absence of legacy unprefixed class, exact 4 ds-fm-row count, all 4 condition titles, jump anchor to #eg-falsifiability, exact 3 occurrences of '0 / N met' (conditions 1, 3, 4), sub-status text presence (2 attempted, imports globe radius, OPEN-001), absence of standalone 'pending' status-num, 1.2rem font-size CSS rule, --ink-2 sub-status color rule, Overview visual order via positional comparison of class indices, sub-0.01% factual fix presence, sub-meter wording absence. The visual-order assertion uses string positions within a slice up to 'class="ds-tab-content" id="evaluate"' — this anchor is resilient to changes elsewhere in the document. No obvious gaps. The test additions are robust.
p4_test_block.potential_brittleness: The status-num 1.2rem regex matches the literal CSS string font-size:1.2rem within a non-greedy property block. If a future EXP rearranges CSS property order within the same selector, the regex still matches as long as font-size:1.2rem is present. Safe.

fuse_side_patch_review.overall_patch_quality: High. The patches are well-scoped, the audit_findings document the rationale clearly, the WCAG arithmetic is sound, the factual fix is correct, and the tests are comprehensive. The substantive review surfaces no blocking content issues — the only blocker is the integration-drift operational failure flagged in H1.

text_fingerprint.note: Standard text_fingerprint fields (claim_length, finding_length, etc.) do not apply — this is an integration-drift review of a section, not a WIN data review.

Operator-rescue precedent commit 5a29442 verified via git log — 'Operator rescue: ship generate-html.js drift (EXP-255/256/257/261/264)' on 2026-04-27 19:48

WCAG large-text threshold of 14pt bold = 18.67px verified against W3C TR/WCAG21 1.4.3 contrast minimum

summary_for_decider: BLOCKING: queue_id 280 cannot be advanced as 'reviewed and integrated' until the EXP-271 source patches actually land in origin/main. The decider's INTEGRATED claim from 2026-04-28T01:28 is contradicted by origin/main HEAD as of 2026-04-29T02:30 — 25 hours of drift. ISS-1631..ISS-1638 are closed in the issue tracker but their fixes are not in production. ACTION REQUIRED: (1) operator rescue commit lifting FUSE generate-html.js + test.js verbatim, (2) `node build.js` to regenerate docs/index.html, (3) `node test.js` to verify 3501-passing baseline, (4) push, (5) once landed, the curmudgeon will re-review the actual integrated content against EXP-271 expectations under a new queue_id. The substantive patch review (fuse_side_patch_review section) finds no blocking content issues — patches will land cleanly when the push succeeds.
followup_recommendations: Decider push-failure block should detect non-zero exit on git push and write a high-priority entry to monitor/decisions/attention-inbox.json so the next decider run notices the drift and re-attempts. Currently the failed-push branch dumps to FUSE for workspace-sync rescue, but workspace-sync's NEVER_PUSH list excludes source code, so the drift is silent until the curmudgeon catches it on the priority-queue review (here, 25 hours later).

Consider a daily-or-twice-daily integrity job that diffs decider's 'INTEGRATED' claims in the latest daily-report against origin/main and flags drift. A 25-hour lag between claim and rescue is too long for a thesis-driven public site.

If structural fix is preferred over serial operator rescues: extend universal-pusher to source code with a more careful predicate (e.g., only push generate-html.js / test.js when git-clone HEAD timestamp predates the FUSE mtime AND the FUSE file passes node test.js cleanly). The NEVER_PUSH ban is a safety blanket but currently leaves a real gap.
